data.title: Final horizontal arm of Sagrada Familia's cross installed, with 17-meter-tall cross bringing total height to 172 meters.
data.description: The last component yet to be added is the upper arm of the cross, featuring Agnus Dei, a sculpture crafted by Italian artist Andrea Mastrovito. Upon completion, this arm will top off the Tower of Jesus Christ, boosting the Sagrada Família's ultimate height to 172 meters. This final installation will wrap up the construction of the magnificent monument.

data.title: TRM Equity sells majority stake of Wellman Dynamics to J.F. Lehman & Co. in 2026.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, a private equity firm called TRM Equity II announced the successful sale of a majority stake in Wellman Dynamics to J.F. Lehman & Company. TRM specializes in control investments in special situations within the lower middle market. The exact financial details of the transaction were not disclosed to the public.
Donald Trump participates in a signing ceremony in the Oval Office

data.title: Bitcoin peaks at $97,500 with 2% rise in Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin; 149,828 traders liquidated for $848.51 million.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin surged to $97,500 briefly on Wednesday due to increased positive market sentiment, leading to a rise in liquidations and heightened demand for ETFs. The current prices for cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $97,561.89, Ethereum at $3,387.58, Solana at $147.07, XRP at $2.15, Dogecoin at $0.1488, and Shiba Inu at $0.058829. In the last 24 hours, 149,828 traders were liquidated, resulting in a total of $848.51 million in losses. Among the top gainers in the past day are Dash, Internet Computer, and Monero. Notable news includes developments involving the crypto custodian company BitGo. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

Flight radar: ‘German authorities have issued a new NOTAM cautioning German airlines from entering Iranian airspace. Lufthansa was scheduled to resume flights to Tehran on Friday. The airline last operated the route in June 2025.’

data.title: Bitcoin encounters major barrier at $96,000 as long-term holders reduce selling. LTH profit-taking down to 12,800 BTC weekly from 100,000 BTC.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently encountering significant resistance at the $96,000 level, which is where buyers from Q2 2025 amassed. According to data from Glassnode, long-term holder profit-taking has decreased substantially from 100,000 BTC to 12,800 BTC on a weekly basis.

data.title: SAMHSA cuts hundreds of grants abruptly, impacting mental health and substance use services nationwide.
data.description: Numerous grantees faced termination yesterday from SAMHSA, affecting mental health and substance use services nationwide. The cuts will add strain to these critical services, impacting hundreds of organizations. The termination letters were sent late yesterday to inform the grantees of the funding cuts. This development is anticipated to impede access to essential support for individuals struggling with mental health and substance use disorders.

data.title: Highway 1 reopens early at Regent's Slide; travelers invited back to Big Sur and SLO CAL-Monterey coastlines at 12:00 PM.
data.description: Renowned coastal highway to be accessible from 12:00 PM today, reopening earlier than expected. Visitors are welcomed back to the famed Big Sur and SLO CAL–Monterey coastlines. The reopening, occurring on January 14, 2026, comes as a pleasant surprise, allowing travelers to explore the picturesque locales of SAN LUIS OBISPO and MONTEREY in California. The news encourages tourists to experience the beauty of the region following the reopening of this iconic route.

data.title: 30 bus workers at Zum Transportation in California join Teamsters Local 186, preparing to negotiate contract.
data.description: 30 bus workers employed at Zum Transportation in California have made the decision to join Teamsters Local 186. The vote was in favor of this move and the workers are now gearing up to negotiate for a robust Teamsters contract. This decision was made on January 14, 2026, in Goleta, California.

data.description: Many companies are shifting towards artificial intelligence and high-performance computing as they repurpose their infrastructure away from cryptocurrency mining.

data.title: Urban Meyer joins Higginbotham to advise on expanding athletics practice with NIL insurance for college sports to protect athlete compensation commitments.
data.description: A firm based in Fort Worth, Texas called Higginbotham recently announced on January 14, 2026, the launch of new coverage options geared towards college programs and donor groups. These options are designed to safeguard the compensation commitments made to modern athletes. Higginbotham, known as a diversified insurance brokerage and financial services company, is rolling out a program known as NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) to cater to this need. This initiative aims to provide protection for the financial welfare of current athletes in college programs.
